response:
Here are comprehensive unit tests for the `is_balanced_parens` function using pytest. The tests cover happy paths, edge cases, and error cases:

```python
import pytest

def test_is_balanced_parens_happy_path():
    # Test with balanced parentheses
    assert is_balanced_parens("()") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("()[]{}") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("({[]})") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("(())") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("([{}])") == True

def test_is_balanced_parens_unbalanced():
    # Test with unbalanced parentheses
    assert is_balanced_parens("(") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens(")") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("([)]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("(]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("([{})") == False

def test_is_balanced_parens_empty_string():
    # Test with empty string
    assert is_balanced_parens("") == True

def test_is_balanced_parens_mixed_characters():
    # Test with mixed characters (only parentheses)
    assert is_balened_parens("a(b)c[d]e{f}g") == True
    assert is_balened_parens("a(b)c[d)e{f}g") == False

def test_is_balanced_parens_nested_parens():
    # Test with deeply nested parentheses
    assert is_balanced_parens("(((())))") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("((())))") == False

def test_is_balanced_parens_multiple_types():
    # Test with multiple types of parentheses
    assert is_balanced_parens("({[]})") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("({[}])") == False

def test_is_balanced_parens_single_type():
    # Test with only one type of parentheses
    assert is_balanced_parens("((()))") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("((()") == False

def test_is_balanced_parens_non_parenthesis_characters():
    # Test with non-parenthesis characters
    assert is_balanced_parens("abcdef") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("a(b)c") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("a(b)c)d") == False

def test_is_balanced_parens_whitespace():
    # Test with whitespace characters
    assert is_balanced_parens(" ( ) ") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("([{ }])") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("([{ }]") == False

def test_is_balanced_parens_large_input():
    # Test with a large input string
    large_balanced = "()[]{}((())){{}}[[ ]]"
    assert is_balanced_parens(large_balanced) == True

    large_unbalanced = "([)]"
    assert is_balanced_parens(large_unbalanced) == False

def test_is_balanced_parens_invalid_characters():
    # Test with invalid characters (not parentheses)
    assert is_balanced_parens("a@b#c") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("a@b#c)d") == False

# Note: There was a typo in the test function name (is_balened_parens)
# This should be corrected to is_balanced_parens in the actual test file
```

There's a small typo in the test function name in the provided code (it says `is_balened_parens` instead of `is_balanced_parens`). Here's the corrected version of the test function name:

```python
def test_is_balanced_parens_mixed_characters():
    # Test with mixed characters (only parentheses)
    assert is_balanced_parens("a(b)c[d]e{f}g") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("a(b)c[d)e{f}g") == False
```
